tape: Risk-off deepened since the last scan: S&P -0.7%, Nasdaq -1.4%, Dow -0.2% as the US-Iran conflict escalated further and SK Hynix tumbled ~15% dragging chips; VIX jumped +12.8% to 16.96 (from 15.03 last hour) - still well off panic levels but the earlier de-escalation read has reversed.

alerts.headline: Reverse-merger pop continuing/extending: now reported +75.7% (up from +61.7% last hour) on the Serapha Bio all-stock merger (combined co. renames to Serapha, trades as AATD, expected close Q4 2026); pre-merger BOLD holders end up with only ~3.7% of the combined company plus a one-time $44-48M cash dividend, funded partly by a $230M PIPE. Still a distressed-merger structure play, not organic strength - do not chase.
Susquehanna trimmed PT to $7 from $8 (still Positive rating); incremental negative but the $1B Equinor backlog and insider buying remain the core support. No new fundamental change.
Jefferies trimmed PT to $22 from $22.50 (still Buy); minor, no new fundamental driver beyond the known debt payoff and $1B+ capacity contracts.

Third consecutive snapshot where generic search surfaces a stale/mismatched quote (~$11.50, -3.8%) vs. broker's actual ~$13.42 flat. Recurring search-engine caching issue, not a real move - no confirmed news catalyst.
No fresh catalyst; $100M buyback authorization and Caris Detect launch remain the latest known news. Quote roughly matches broker (~$16.99 vs broker $16.74).
No new news; CEO's $968K insider buy (7/7) and the ~$1.9B Vacuumschmelze acquisition remain the latest known catalysts.
Ticker ambiguity persists (search keeps surfacing NetSol/NTWK); no confirmed company-specific news found today for the held NTSK position.
No new news; BLA filing, Wainwright Buy initiation, and 7/8 director buy remain the latest catalysts. Baird's $5 PT cut (6/30) still the only incremental negative.
No fresh news; late-June PT hikes (Leerink/Guggenheim/BTIG/Morgan Stanley/Wainwright) remain the latest known catalysts.

No fresh news; a 'Kenneth Gaglione named Chairman/CEO' headline surfaced in search but dates to Nov 24 2025 - stale, not today's news. DLA refrigerant contract extension remains the latest real catalyst.
No fresh news; the Mandela Digital/Mandela Dollar tech-partner deal (7/9) remains the latest, now 4 days old.

movers.note: Reverse-merger pop extending to +75.7% on the Serapha Bio deal; distressed-structure pop, not fundamental strength.
